source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/TimeSeries/ARIMAAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- D5 LOOSE smoke oracle (self-computed from the ported C++ core, NOT yet emitter-verified; D6 tightens to exact). ARIMAAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over an ARIMA(1,1,1) model, then builds the posterior forecast ensemble (mode/mean/credible band). training_time_steps pinned to 15; curve_length = 20 + 3 = 23. NOTE (D5): unlike the AR/MA/mixture/competing-risk/point-process smoke fixtures (bit-stable across the C++ ctest and the R/Python package builds), this 4-parameter differenced model's SHORT MCMC trajectory is chaotic enough that a single floating-point reassociation flips an accept/reject and the point estimate / AIC / RMSE diverge across builds -- so this fixture asserts ONLY the build-stable STRUCTURAL invariants (curve length + the deterministic first-step forecast, which equals the first observed value); the exact seeded trajectory scalars are deferred to D6's controlled-build emitter oracle. thinning_interval pinned to 1. Short run (iterations 100, output_length 400).
